title: Mia and the Weather Machine
description: When AI predicts a flood three days before it hits, Mia and her uncle have to decide — do they trust the machine?

steps.content: Mia is ten and lives in a village in the Philippines. Every monsoon season the floods come — it's something everyone just accepts. But this year, her uncle the fisherman gets access to a new AI weather prediction app built by a local nonprofit. It doesn't just say 'rain tomorrow.' It predicts storm surges three days ahead using satellite data and ocean temperatures.

steps.question: How can an AI predict a flood better than a local radio station?
options: AI makes things up to sound smart
AI processes huge amounts of satellite, ocean, and weather data that humans can't read that fast
AI is always connected to the weather

steps.content: The app sends an unusual warning — worse than the radio forecast. Mia's uncle isn't sure who to trust. Mia investigates: the app looks at ocean temperature, wind patterns, and historical flood data from the last 50 years all at once. The radio station only checks local sensors.

steps.instruction: The AI warning contradicts the radio. What should Mia's uncle do?
options: Ignore the app — radio has always been reliable
Prepare early based on the AI warning and explain why to neighbours

steps.content: They prepared early. The flood came — worse than the radio predicted, but their family was ready. Mia started teaching the app to the elderly women in her village who didn't have smartphones — she created a 'weather board' to relay the AI's predictions in plain language. AI is a tool. She decided what it was for.
